capital.summary: Arizona State University broke ground on its new ASU Health building in downtown Phoenix, a 175,000-square-foot facility that will house the John Shufeldt School of Medicine and Medical Engineering, the School of Technology for Public Health, the ASU Health Observatory, and programs from the Edson College of Nursing and Health Innovation and the College of Health Solutions.
capital.timeline: Construction expected to begin July 2026 and finish before the start of the fall 2028 semester.
capital.why_it_matters: This new medical and health sciences facility will require a wide range of lab supplies, reagents, consumables, and scientific equipment for its numerous traditional and virtual reality classrooms, health care simulation and skills labs, and medical-technology innovation suite.

funding.summary: Arizona State University received a $47.5 million investment to establish a pioneering research, development, and prototyping lab focused on next-generation wafer manufacturing and chip packaging.
funding.use_of_proceeds: To enable a first-of-its-kind research, development and prototyping lab to support next-generation wafer manufacturing and chip packaging.
funding.why_it_matters: This investment in a new lab for semiconductor manufacturing and packaging will drive demand for specialized lab equipment, reagents, and consumables for research and prototyping.

project.summary: Construction has begun on Arizona State University's new Health Headquarters building in the downtown Phoenix Bioscience Core, which will house the John Shufeldt School of Medicine and Medical Engineering, and a new School of Technology for Public Health.
project.project_name: ASU Health Headquarters Building / John Shufeldt School of Medicine and Medical Engineering / School of Technology for Public Health
project.scope: The approximately 175,000-square-foot, five-story facility will integrate education, research, clinical collaboration, and innovation, focusing on advancing health outcomes by blending medicine, engineering, technology, and humanities, and integrating digital technology and data-driven decisions for public health.
project.timeline: Construction is expected to complete in Summer 2028, with classes beginning in Fall 2028.
project.why_it_matters: The establishment of new medical and public health engineering schools within this facility will create significant long-term demand for a wide range of lab supplies, reagents, consumables, and scientific equipment for research and educational purposes.

breakthrough.summary: Arizona State University's Barbara Smith, an associate professor of biomedical engineering, was honored with the Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists and Engineers (PECASE) in 2025, the highest honor bestowed by the U.S. government on outstanding early-career researchers. The award recognizes her impact in science and dedication to mentorship, education, and shaping the next generation of engineers, particularly for her efforts in developing brain imaging technologies and identifying disease markers for neurodegenerative diseases.
breakthrough.department_or_lab: School of Biological and Health Systems Engineering, Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering
breakthrough.award_or_discovery: Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists and Engineers (PECASE)
breakthrough.significance: PECASE is the highest honor bestowed by the United States government on outstanding early-career researchers. Her work is paving the way for a greater foundational understanding of how neurodegenerative diseases function.
breakthrough.why_it_matters: This award for pioneering work in neurodegenerative diagnostics signals high-impact lab activity requiring specialized brain imaging equipment, biochemical reagents, and consumables for biomarker identification and analysis.

breakthrough.summary: S.-H. Dan Shim, a professor of geosciences at Arizona State University's School of Earth and Space Exploration, has been named a 2026 Guggenheim Fellow by the John Simon Guggenheim Memorial Foundation. This recognition is for his interdisciplinary work bridging earth science and astrophysics, where he recreates planetary conditions in the laboratory using powerful X-rays and lasers.
breakthrough.department_or_lab: School of Earth and Space Exploration
breakthrough.significance: The Guggenheim Fellowship recognizes exceptional prior career achievement and outstanding promise for future contributions, and Shim's work is expanding the understanding of planetary systems and their conditions.
breakthrough.why_it_matters: His experimental research involving the recreation of planetary conditions in the lab indicates a demand for high-pressure/temperature equipment, advanced spectroscopic tools, and specialized materials for geosciences and astrophysics research.

breakthrough.summary: Dr. Sarah H. Lisanby, founding dean of the Shufeldt School at Arizona State University, has been elected to the American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering College of Fellows. This honor recognizes her pioneering treatments for depression, which bridge medicine and engineering.
breakthrough.award_or_discovery: Election to American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering College of Fellows
breakthrough.significance: Fellows include Nobel Prize and Presidential Medal of Science winners, representing the top 2% of medical and biological engineers, highlighting her significant contributions to developing brain stimulation therapies.
breakthrough.why_it_matters: Her recognition for pioneering brain stimulation therapies signals continued high-impact research in neuro-engineering and medical device development, driving demand for specialized electronics, biological reagents, and clinical research consumables.
